metadata.name: TableOfContents
metadata.description: Compact, accessible 'On this page' navigation that lists the section anchors of the current screen, visually emphasizes the currently in-view section, and scrolls to the target section on click. Used on long-form content pages (/learn/installation, /learn/hello-world, and eventually /reference/* and /guides/*). Web-only v1: the React converter registers under rjui_tools/lib/react/converters/extensions/.

items.name: items
items.description: Required. Ordered list of entries to render. Each TocItem has: id (String, stable identifier used as React key and anchor fallback), label (String, user-visible text; may be a strings.json key or a pre-resolved string — implementer decides), anchor (String, the DOM fragment id to scroll to, without the leading '#'), level (Int?, 1 or 2, defaults to 1, drives nesting indentation). Authored by the page spec; v1 does NOT auto-extract from DOM headings.

items.name: title
items.description: Optional heading rendered above the list (e.g. 'On this page'). When omitted, the <nav> has no caption.

items.name: sticky
items.description: When true, the component uses position: sticky with top: stickyOffset under a sufficiently tall viewport. Responsive behavior (stack at narrow widths) is the React component's decision. Defaults to true.

items.name: stickyOffset
items.description: Offset from viewport top, in pixels, used when sticky is true so the TOC doesn't overlap a site header. Defaults to 80.

items.name: activeAnchor
items.description: Binding-only input. When present, the row whose anchor matches this value is emphasized. When absent, the React component runs an IntersectionObserver to self-compute the active anchor. Supply this prop only when the consumer wants authoritative external control (e.g. router-scroll integration).

items.name: maxDepth
items.description: Items whose level is greater than maxDepth are filtered out before rendering. Allows reusing one rich source list to render a shallow TOC. Defaults to 2.

notes: TocItem is an inline structural type described in the 'items' prop above; it is not a standalone layout component and has no converter of its own.
Color, padding, font-size, and narrow-viewport breakpoints are intentionally unspecified and are the React component author's call; this spec only pins behavior and data shape.

Deferred for a later revision (NOT in v1): autoScan (Bool?) to extract headings from the rendered DOM, collapsible (Bool?) to expand/collapse a level-1 section's level-2 children, and any reading-position / progress-bar indicator.
TableOfContents is a leaf component. Children passed in a Layout JSON are ignored. All content comes from the 'items' prop.

components.description: Root <nav aria-label='On this page'> container wrapping the optional title and the ordered anchor list.

internalStates.name: computedActiveAnchor
internalStates.description: Self-computed active anchor produced by the React component's IntersectionObserver when the 'activeAnchor' prop is not supplied. Implementation detail; not part of the public props contract.

exposedEvents.name: onSelect
exposedEvents.description: Fired when the user clicks an item. The component invokes onSelect(anchor) first (if wired) and then performs the native scroll-to-anchor. Optional: when not wired, default scroll behavior still runs.

example.description: Minimal TableOfContents usage inside a /learn/installation Layout JSON. Authored items mirror the page's heading structure; level 2 rows are filtered out if maxDepth is set to 1.

notes: Spec-first discipline (docs/plans/02-tech-stack.md §6.1): this file MUST exist and validate before .jsonui-doc-rules.json registers 'TableOfContents' in componentTypes.screen; the converter is then generated via `jui g converter --from tableofcontents.component.json` (implementer step — not authored here).

Sticky rationale: a sticky TOC keeps navigation in view on long-form pages without a persistent sidebar on narrow viewports. The component itself decides how to collapse below the breakpoint; this spec does not pin a pixel value.

Scroll-spy rationale: 'activeAnchor' as an optional external input keeps the component self-sufficient (IntersectionObserver fallback) while still allowing integrations (router hash sync, nested scroll containers) to take over when needed.

Explicit NOT-YET (so the next implementer doesn't re-derive): no DOM auto-scan of headings, no collapsible nesting, no reading-position/progress-bar indicator. Each would be a later additive prop with its own ADR.

Related files (not authored here): jsonui-doc-web/rjui_tools/lib/react/converters/extensions/table_of_contents_converter.rb (converter scaffold target), jsonui-doc-web/rjui_tools/lib/react/converters/extensions/attribute_definitions/TableOfContents.json (attribute-def sidecar, scaffold target), jsonui-doc-web/src/components/extensions/TableOfContents.tsx (React component target).
